questions.question: What conservation projects or events are currently advertised?
questions.answer: The Community Compost program is an ongoing conservation initiative where Pound Ridge residents can drop off organic food waste at the Town's Recycling Center, 290 Stone Hill Road, on Wednesdays and Saturdays from 8 a.m.–noon. Free Compost Kits are available from the Recreation Department at the Town House. Additionally, Pound Ridge Proud Day includes beautification projects such as planting and maintaining green spaces, painting, and general cleanup, open to residents of all ages and community groups.
nextPrompts: Is there a scheduled date and registration process for Pound Ridge Proud Day 2025/2026?

questions.question: What documentation or photo/videos showing past projects exists online?
questions.answer: The Town of Poundridge Conservation Board's community compost page references thumbnail images that can be clicked to view a slideshow, suggesting some photo documentation exists online at https://www.townofpoundridge.com/conservationboard/community-compost. No other specific photo, video, or project documentation is described in the provided sources.
